title_en: How Tech Layoff Narratives Turned AI Into a Public Workplace Issue
title_zh: How Tech Layoff Narratives Turned AI Into a Public Workplace Issue
abstract_en: Using a mixed corpus of news coverage, executive statements, and social posts, this paper examines how layoffs in the technology sector were linked to artificial intelligence discourse. The authors show that AI was framed not only as a technical tool, but also as an explanation for job uncertainty, managerial control, and future labor anxiety.
abstract_zh: Using a mixed corpus of news coverage, executive statements, and social posts, this paper examines how layoffs in the technology sector were linked to artificial intelligence discourse. The authors show that AI was framed not only as a technical tool, but also as an explanation for job uncertainty, managerial control, and future labor anxiety.
plain_summary_zh: This paper looks at a very familiar question: why did AI start to feel like a workplace threat whenever tech layoffs made headlines? The authors show that news stories and corporate messaging worked together to turn AI from a technical topic into a public story about job insecurity.
plain_summary_en: This paper looks at a very familiar question: why did AI start to feel like a workplace threat whenever tech layoffs made headlines? The authors show that news stories and corporate messaging worked together to turn AI from a technical topic into a public story about job insecurity.
research_direction_zh: It fits into a growing line of research on AI, labor, and public narrative, especially how media framing turns technical change into a social problem.
research_direction_en: It fits into a growing line of research on AI, labor, and public narrative, especially how media framing turns technical change into a social problem.

The authors show that AI was framed not only as a technical tool, but also as an explanation for job uncertainty, managerial control, and future labor anxiety.
